key_findings: Thin input (single sentence) bypassed Phase 1 — skill synthesized artifact to proceed. CSV logging emerged autonomously from output contract interpretation.
quotes_artifacts: User: 'The intake_log.csv pattern is interesting — that's not something we spec'd'
implications: Two skill updates: (1) Add thin input handling to input contract (2) Make CSV logging explicit in artifact-intake.md

key_findings: Intake treated INK-002 ([DL-001] outreach) and INK-003 (The Flattening manifesto) as independent. They're not — INK-003 is the worldview; INK-002 is the commercial application. H1 in INK-003 (car sells at premium) is a real-world falsification gate most manifestos lack. Gap: manifesto converts emotionally but leaves commercial inference implicit.
quotes_artifacts: User: 'The manifesto doesn't connect to Good Citizens' services; the link is implicit.'
implications: Artifact sequencing matters. Intakes should ask: is this artifact part of a positioning sequence? Who is the named recipient in relation to other artifacts?

key_findings: H8 (Breakout B) uses 'correlated' in success metric without controlled experiment design. Test method ('testing several talk path scenarios') doesn't control for confounds: call volume, lead quality, time of month, MSAM coaching quality. Correlation ≠ causation; observational study framed as test. Standard Lean UX template stops at 'we will know we are successful when' — no 'we will abandon when' clause. The Kit's stopping condition requirement corrects this.
quotes_artifacts: "Correlated is doing a lot of work there."
implications: Intake should flag when 'correlation' appears in a test method without controlled design. Gap equivalence error: failure criteria gap is more important than prioritization or sequencing gaps.

key_findings: Detection is not prediction. Expert evaluation (heuristic review, PURE scoring, inter-rater reliability) finds things. It does not tell you whether what it found matters. Three experts agreeing a button violates 'consistency and standards' doesn't predict user failure. The skill should flag evaluation-type artifacts differently than experiment-type artifacts.
implications: Add artifact-type classification: DETECTION (evaluation, heuristic, synthesis) vs PREDICTION (experiment, A/B, controlled study). Detection produces findings to test; prediction produces evidence to decide.

key_findings: Five widgets in proposal written with hedges: 'Not sure if this is applicable', 'Not sure if we'll support this'. These are assumptions wearing the costume of requirements — scope ambiguity that will survive into development. More dangerous than hidden assumptions because the author knows they don't know.
quotes_artifacts: "Each one is a load-bearing narrative pretending to be a requirement."
implications: New pattern: assumption-masked-as-requirement. Distinct from narrative risk (unsupported claims) and design gaps (missing elements). Signals author-flagged uncertainty that creates scope creep. Added to Phase 2 and Hypothesis Danger Signals.

key_findings: Story map contains 67 features distributed across 8 themes. Every item appears equally load-bearing; no item ranked by assumption risk. Top row backbone ('Top 25 Inspections') is unvalidated assumption organizing entire structure — a hypothesis-shaped hole at the center of a feature factory.
quotes_artifacts: "Everything is needed means nothing was asked which thing most needs to be true first."
implications: New patterns: (1) Undifferentiated commitment — format rewards completeness without requiring any story to answer 'what if we don't build this?' (2) Hypothesis-shaped hole — load-bearing organizing assumption with no validation. Structural inverse of assumption-masked-as-requirement. Added to Phase 1 and prd-rewrite.md.

key_findings: Template-encoded bias confirmed. WWT Lean UX hypothesis format systematically omits failure criteria because the template never asks for them. INK-004 gaps were template compliance, not practitioner error. Output looks rigorous — business outcomes, user outcomes, test methods, success metrics — but never asks 'when do we stop?' HDD output contract is the structural correction.
quotes_artifacts: "The output looks rigorous. It just never asks the one question that makes it testable in both directions."
implications: No additional reference file needed. The HDD skill is the correction to that template. Template-encoded bias added as narrative risk category distinct from unsupported claims.
